hero_subtitle: SFTY helps crypto exchanges operationalize MAS-specific rules by connecting provider outputs into audit-ready workflows that align onboarding, Travel Rule, case management, and regulatory updates.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_description: Exchanges face complex challenges aligning onboarding, Travel Rule, and screening workflows with MAS guidelines across jurisdictions. This results in slow compliance processes, fragmented evidence, and difficulty maintaining a clear audit trail for regulatory review readiness.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY centralizes provider data, regulatory rules and case management into a single operational layer that streamlines MAS-specific onboarding, Travel Rule handling and audit-ready evidence for crypto exchanges.
workflow_title: From provider checks to MAS compliance decisions
workflow_description: SFTY processes jurisdiction-specific provider outputs through rules, case creation, review, evidence preservation and reporting to support MAS compliance workflows for exchanges.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond fragmented checks. Structured compliance control
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects jurisdiction-specific provider outputs into audit-ready workflows, decisions, evidence, and reporting to support consistent MAS exchange compliance operations.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports crypto exchanges with operational workflows that preserve audit evidence, enable provider orchestration and strengthen bank due diligence in regulated environments.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY support MAS exchange compliance workflows
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how case workflows support MAS compliance reviews
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit evidence supports MAS regulatory inspections
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Explain how provider orchestration handles jurisdiction-specific rules
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for MAS exchange compliance?
faq_1_answer: SFTY structures compliance workflows for MAS-regulated exchanges, connecting provider outputs into cases, decisions, evidence and reports to help meet Singapore’s jurisdiction-specific requirements.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY support MAS onboarding workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY orchestrates onboarding by linking KYC, KYB and screening outputs into consistent MAS-aligned review workflows with risk-based routing and audit trails.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves case history, decision logs and supporting evidence to provide an audit-ready trail compliant with MAS supervisory expectations.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing compliance tools?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY connects existing KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one controlled compliance operating layer without disrupting current tools.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace existing compliance providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace providers but integrates their outputs into structured workflows, enhancing multi-jurisdictional compliance and operational efficiency.
final_description: Review how SFTY connects providers and policies into audit-ready cases, regulatory rule alignment, Travel Rule handling and onboarding processes.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
